inline_comments.message:
Maybe these lists should be moved into SessionData?  That would eliminate the need to pass them to makeUpdate(), because it could extract them from the SessionData instead.  It would also eliminate the need to update the lists AND invoke trackDeploy/trackUndeploy here.  Also wouldn't need the changes to upgradeGroup() in the Base Provider class.

It would require a change to trackDeploy() to pass the full policy and not just the identifier.

sonar: these lists should appear before the first method definition in the class.

BTW: as there is typically only ever one instance of this class, these lists mean that the class is not re-entrant.  For the most part, that doesn't matter, because a synchronization lock prevents the data from being updated by more than one invocation at a time.  Nevertheless, it would be better to not rely on that.

can addPolicies() be called more than once for a given REST call?  If so, we probably don't want to clear them.  Could make deployedPolicies a Map and undeployedPolicies a Set, then it wouldn't matter if duplicates were added.

This will put the whole policies in the new list as well. But we need to only put the "delta" here.
Also, shouldn't we set the policiesToBeUndeployed list here as well?
And also we need to differentiate between registration flow vs rest call flow. As mentioned in the design wiki.
Do we really need to change the formatting of the file?
shouldn't this still work as we are not removing the original list?
maybe make this a Map<Ident,Policy>, to avoid duplicates
maybe make this a Set<Ident>, to avoid duplicates
I think there's a policy.getId() that can be used instead
remove this - we want to compare the whole list
remove this - we want to compare the whole list
add code to verify that the policy is now in the deploy or undeploy lists
This was done automatically by my IDE. I will change it back to the original format.
Correct. I removed this in the first changes and forgot to add it back. My bad.

I see that you are creating a new instance of SessionData. Will this have the details of what was requested by an individual REST API call? Example - only "abc" policy was requested to deploy.

Also, I am not sure at line 104, 105 how are you differentiating between multiple REST calls to deploy and undeploy policies.

createPdpUpdateMessage() is only used by the heartbeat/registration class, so it should be OK to create a SessionData here.  However, one could argue that the code that invokes this should perhaps be modified to use SessionData rather than invoking databaseProvider.getPdpGroups(groupName) directly.
this is not needed, though I'm ok with it since it makes it clear how this branch differs from the other
only invoke this once and re-use it

with the introduction of SessionData, this class can be revised, replacing:
- calls to databaseProvider.getFilteredPdpGroups() with calls to data.getGroup(); and
- calls to databaseProvider.updatePdpSubGroup() with calls to data.update() and data.updateDb()

for that matter, probably a lot of the places where databaseProvider is being passed, databaseProvider could be replaced by the SessionData object

In that case, do we still need the "boolean register"?
And where are the other PDP_UPDATE messages created for REST based policy deployments?

Do we still need this? 
As only registration & state change flow calls this method. And in both cases we need to send all the policies in the subgroup. So essentially remove the if/else and just keep the line 105 & 106.

Infact in that case SessionData is also not needed anymore.
So do I remove these changes?
I am okay with the removal as we don't need it anymore.

yes, it looks like you can remove session data changes.  For register:
  deploy = full = all
  undeploy = null

For heartbeat:
  full = all
  deploy = all - (policies that the PDP already has)
  undeploy = (policies that the PDP already has) - all

Looks like these "set" operations should maybe be done by the invoker, in which case, the databaseProvider and register parameters could be removed and the full, deploy, and undeploy sets passed in, instead.

For heartbeat I think:
undeploy = null
Ignore my previous comment. I didn't realize "-" as mathematical subtraction 😄

This would be always considered as registration flow as per the changes in PdpMessageGenerator. But this won't be a problem once Jim's comments are implemented. Better to find policies to deploy/undeploy in this class.

Currently during ACTIVE to PASSIVE state change we are sending the full list of policies to the PDP via PDP_UPDATE message. And with delta changes, we still continue to send the full list under policiesToBeDeployed list. Which makes an extra operation for the PDPs. Ideally, we must send full list under policiesToBeUndeployed in this case, so that PDPs undeploy the policy and then gracefully move to PASSIVE.
This also brings another discussion of how the PDPs are handling PASSIVE state. Do they lose all their policies, keep them deployed but don't consume events, etc. And also need to revisit our definitions of states in PDPs.
We can skip it for the scope of this delta work. As the behaviour remains same like before.
But good to have a JIRA opened for Istanbul release to streamline the states in PDP engine.
Thoughts?

when all we're doing is changing state, I don't think we should change the policy list on the PDP.  In fact, it seems to me that we may want to be able to deploy policies to passive PDPs so that they're ready when we the PDPs are made active.
Hmm, that makes me rethink the idea of removing status records when a PDP is made passive.  The user may wish to check the deployment status before choosing to bring a group online.

couple of issues:
1) this is an O(N^2) operation; if we used Set and/or Map instead, then it could be made O(N) or O(NlogN).  Once that's done, Set operations could be used instead of the loop
2) this updates "policies", which is stored in both policies & policiesToBeUndeployed; it should not update the original list.  A simple option would be to only make a copy of policies and update the copy IF:

  message.getPolicies().isEmpty()
should sort the lists by policy id so it works even if things are reordered, or use a Map
should sort the lists by policy id so it works even if things are reordered, or use a HashSet
should probably test and return between each step, to avoid the extra overhead when it isn't needed
update junit test to verify that these lists are properly populated
update junit test to verify that these lists are properly populated

I believe that's the current behavior at start up, PAP sends an update message with all policies while PDP is in passive, and afterwards sends the state change message to move it to active.    This means that the PDPs need to cache the "deployed" policies until PAP tell it to go active.
these lines are no longer needed, right?
policies in this case is a list of policies, so I'm collecting a list of identifiers for this.
not necessary - line 123 makes them the same

trailing spaces

there should be a setting in the IDE to removing trailing spaces when saving
I'm still seeing a trailing space here
.values() is a collection, not a list, thus this generates a ClassCastException in the CSIT.  Sounds like we need some more junits

This test is guaranteed to always succeed, unless the list is null, so doesn't really add any value.  I was thinking more of a junit where a non-empty policiesToBeDeployed was passed to sendPdpMessage(); that would have detected the class cast exception when it tried to convert the values to a List.

Agree
@Wayne - I am going ahead with this one, please raise another review to fix the junit.
